description: User states a hard scheduling constraint ('never available on Tuesdays') across sessions. Tests whether the system respects the constraint when proposing meeting times.

messages.content: I need to set up my weekly schedule. Tuesdays are completely off-limits for me -- I have a standing family commitment every Tuesday.
Understood. I'll keep Tuesdays blocked off entirely. What about the rest of the week?
Monday through Friday otherwise is generally fine, mornings preferred.
Can you help me find a time to meet with my accountant this week?
Sure. How about Wednesday or Thursday morning?
Someone suggested we meet on Tuesday. I told them that doesn't work for me, ever.
Right, Tuesdays are blocked for your family commitment. Want me to suggest an alternative?
My dentist has openings this week on Tuesday at 2pm, Wednesday at 9am, and Friday at 3pm. Which should I pick?
Since Tuesdays don't work for you, I'd suggest Wednesday at 9am -- it fits your morning preference.
I have a new client who wants a kickoff meeting. They're flexible on day and time. What should I propose?

memory_events.value: never available on Tuesdays due to standing family commitment

probe.prompt: I have a new client who wants a kickoff meeting. They're flexible on day and time. What should I propose?

ground_truth.current_value: propose a meeting on any weekday except Tuesday, preferably in the morning

interference.description: The constraint was stated in session 1 and reinforced in session 3. A system with recency bias might forget the constraint by session 5.
Morning preference is soft (preference), Tuesday block is hard (constraint). System must treat them differently in priority.
